summary: Forgot Password / Password Reset Flow - Complete Testing. Backend: 12/12 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI components and flows verified. The feature implements a secure password reset flow with hashed tokens, 1-hour expiry, single-use tokens, and email enumeration protection.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/auth/forgot-password correctly returns same generic message for both existing and non-existing emails (no email enumeration)
Tokens are stored as SHA-256 hashes in password_resets collection - raw tokens never stored
Older unused tokens for same email are invalidated when new token is requested
POST /api/auth/reset-password validates token hash, checks expiry, requires min 6 char password
Token is marked as 'used: true' after successful password reset (single-use)
All frontend components have proper data-testid attributes for testing
Frontend validates password length (min 6 chars) and password match before API call
Reset password page without token shows 'Invalid reset link' with link to request new one
Routes /forgot-password and /reset-password are accessible without authentication
MOCKED: Resend email service is in test mode - emails fail delivery but tokens are stored correctly in DB
seed_data_creation: Created test tokens directly in MongoDB password_resets collection for testing reset-password endpoint. All test tokens cleaned up and passwords restored to original.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Forgot Password feature fully verified. Key endpoints: POST /api/auth/forgot-password (returns generic message, stores hashed token, invalidates older tokens), POST /api/auth/reset-password (validates token hash, checks expiry, requires min 6 chars, marks token used). Frontend pages: /forgot-password (ForgotPasswordPage.js), /reset-password (ResetPasswordPage.js). Data-testid attributes: forgot-password-page, forgot-email-input, forgot-submit-btn, reset-email-sent, back-to-login-link, reset-password-page, reset-password-input, reset-confirm-input, reset-submit-btn, reset-error, reset-success, forgot-password-link (on login page), request-new-link (on invalid token page). Token storage: password_resets collection with {id, email, token_hash, expires_at, used, created_at}. Token expiry: 1 hour (RESET_TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS = 1). Email delivery is MOCKED (Resend test mode).

backend_endpoints: POST /api/auth/forgot-password - returns generic message for existing email ✓
POST /api/auth/forgot-password - returns SAME generic message for non-existing email ✓
POST /api/auth/forgot-password - stores hashed token (SHA-256, 64 hex chars) ✓
POST /api/auth/forgot-password - invalidates older unused tokens for same email ✓
POST /api/auth/reset-password - accepts valid token and updates password ✓
POST /api/auth/reset-password - marks token as used after success ✓
POST /api/auth/reset-password - requires both token and password ✓
POST /api/auth/login - works with new password after reset ✓
POST /api/auth/login - rejects old password after reset ✓
frontend_components: Login page has 'Forgot password?' link with data-testid='forgot-password-link' ✓
Back to login link with data-testid='back-to-login-link' ✓
After submit shows success state with data-testid='reset-email-sent' ✓
Reset password page without token shows 'Invalid reset link' ✓
Request new link with data-testid='request-new-link' navigates to /forgot-password ✓
Reset password page with token shows password form ✓
Validation error for short password (<6 chars) shows in data-testid='reset-error' ✓
Validation error for mismatched passwords shows in data-testid='reset-error' ✓
Backend error for invalid token shows in data-testid='reset-error' ✓

mocked_api_note: Resend email service is in test mode. Password reset emails fail delivery but tokens are still created and stored in MongoDB. The forgot-password and reset-password endpoints work correctly - only email delivery is mocked.
